paragraphs.body: When I first started making the non-AI-assisted video, it was honestly pretty tough. I didn't know how to get the animations right, and something that seemed as simple as making the bus move to the stop ended up being a huge time sink. I spent so much time just trying to figure out how to make things move properly on the screen, like the bus pulling up to the station. It felt like I was stuck at square one, having to learn basic animation skills just to make anything happen. This made the whole process slow and frustrating.

But when I used AI, specifically Vyond Go, for the second video, everything became way easier. The AI automatically generated the animations I needed, so I didn’t have to worry about getting the bus to move or the characters to walk—stuff that took me ages before! All I had to do was tweak a few things here and there to match what I wanted. We used the dialogue from the comic we’d created earlier, and I was super happy that the result was exactly what I had in mind. The character expressions, in particular, turned out just like we had hoped. It was so satisfying to see everything come together without the headache.

I think the coolest thing about using AI in video creation is that it allows me to take my ideas and make them real without all the technical hassle. Without AI, I’d have to spend ages learning animation basics before I could even start working on something as simple as the bus scene. It’s kind of annoying because it feels like a huge roadblock between your creativity and what you can actually produce. But AI cuts out that middle step and lets me focus on being creative. What would’ve taken me hours of fiddling around with animations before was done in minutes. Honestly, if I didn’t have AI, I probably would have only finished a basic 10-second animation after all that time!

Now, overall, I’m really pleased with the video Vyond Go helped me make. The voiceovers fit well, and the key moments, like the characters’ facial expressions, came out great. Even the special effects were solid. But the one thing that didn’t quite meet my expectations was the background. It was way too plain and generic, and I had to manually adjust things like trees. The backgrounds didn’t seem to fit the theme or the keywords we had in mind for our project. So, I ended up spending some time tweaking the backgrounds to make them more futuristic, with sleek skyscrapers and a modern look. I think adding those elements gave the video a much better vibe overall, making it feel more aligned with the story we were telling.

In the end, using AI made the whole process so much more enjoyable. Without it, I would’ve been stuck on basic animations for hours, but with AI, I could focus on the creative parts and let the tech handle the rest. There are still a few things I’d like to improve, like the customization of backgrounds, but overall, AI makes video creation way easier and more fun for someone like me who’s still learning. I was able to turn my ideas into something tangible without feeling overwhelmed by technical details, which is honestly the best part.
